title: 10 best herbal creams for gas in stomach

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to bloating and discomfort. This relief from muscle tension allows gases to pass more easily through the digestive system.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in peppermint creams help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can exacerbate symptoms of gas and bloating. By neutralizing free radicals, antioxidants promote a healthier gut environment and alleviate discomfort.

Carminative properties: Peppermint's carminative properties help to eliminate trapped gases from the digestive system by relaxing the muscles and promoting the release of carbon dioxide and other gases. This reduces feelings of bloating and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with gas in stomach because it contains high levels of menthol, which provides rapid relief from digestive discomfort. The menthol in peppermint creams works by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines, allowing gas to pass through the digestive system more easily. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe an irritated stomach lining, reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of calm and relief. This can provide quick and effective relief from bloating and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps alleviate stomach gas by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort.
Menthone: Menthone, a terpene in peppermint, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation that can lead to gas and bloating.
Limonene: Limonene, a monoterpene in peppermint, has carminative properties, which help to expel gas from the stomach and intestines, providing relief from discomfort and bloating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita contain menthol and menthone, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate stomach gas and cramps.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita contain menthol and menthone, similar to the leaves, which helps to relax muscles and reduce stomach discomfort.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita also contain menthol and menthone, making them a useful part for making creams that target stomach gas and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a quantity of fresh menthapiperita leaves by cutting them just above the node with scissors during peak season.
Weigh 200 grams of the harvested leaves and add 1 gram of vitamin e oil to a blender.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the blended leaf mixture and 10 grams of shea butter to the double boiler and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 120 degrees fahrenheit before pouring it into jars.

remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel cream's carminative property helps to release trapped gases from the digestive system, reducing bloating and discomfort by relieving flatulence and promoting smooth digestion.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel's anti-inflammatory property soothes and calms the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, which can become irritated and inflamed due to gas and indigestion, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Digestive stimulant: Fennel cream's digestive stimulant property helps to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating by increasing the production of digestive enzymes and promoting the movement of food through the digestive system.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with gas in stomach because it contains compounds like anethole and estragole, which have natural carminative properties. These properties help to release trapped gas and reduce bloating, promoting a sense of comfort and relief. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties may soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). As a result, fennel creams can be an effective and natural remedy for individuals experiencing stomach gas and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole is a terpene that helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with gas.
Fenchone: Fenchone is a monoterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate gas-related discomfort.
Trans-anethole: Trans-anethole is a terpene that has carminative properties, which means it can help to expel gas from the digestive tract and relieve symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used due to their carminative properties, which help to alleviate gas and reduce discomfort in the stomach.
Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and carminative effects, making them useful in creams for gas relief.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain oils that are often used to create digestive remedies, which can help to alleviate stomach gas and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at dawn to maximize essential oil content.
Steep the harvested foeniculum vulgare in 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 14 days.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then add 50 grams of beeswax to melt.
Heat the mixture to 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes while constantly stirring the cream base.
Allow the foeniculum vulgare cream to cool and solidify before transferring it to a clean glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Ginger's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes helps break down food more efficiently, reducing gas and bloating by promoting proper digestion. This is because it activates the enzyme pepsin, which breaks down proteins into smaller molecules.

Antispasmodic Agent: Ginger's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the stomach, intestines, and digestive tract, reducing spasms that can contribute to gas and bloating. This relaxation helps move food through the digestive system more smoothly.

Inflammation Reducer: Ginger has anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome), including gas and bloating. By reducing inflammation, these compounds promote a healthy gut environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with gas in stomach because it contains properties that aid digestion and relieve discomfort. The cream's ginger extract helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can cause gas and bloating. It also stimulates digestive enzymes, promoting the breakdown of food and preventing the accumulation of gas. Furthermore, ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with gas. This makes ginger creams a natural remedy for gas relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, help reduce gas in the stomach by inhibiting the production of gas-producing enzymes and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.

Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, help alleviate gas and discomfort in the stomach by reducing inflammation, inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscles, and preventing the release of histamine, a chemical that can exacerbate stomach issues.

Terpenoids (specifically Zingiberene and Bisabolol): Terpenoids help reduce gas in the stomach by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, improving digestion, and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to the formation of gas in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of ginger are used because they contain gingerols and shogaols, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties, helping to alleviate stomach discomfort and gas.

Roots: Ginger roots are used because they contain a high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits, making them effective in relieving stomach gas and discomfort.

Buds: Ginger buds are used because they are less fibrous and have a higher concentration of essential oils, making them easier to extract and utilize in creams to provide relief from stomach gas and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizome roots from a trusted supplier.
Grind 10 grams of the harvested zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and melt at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Add the ground zingiber officinale powder and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil to the melted coconut oil mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Carminative property: Lemongrass has carminative properties, which help to relieve bloating and flatulence by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive tract.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds in lemongrass cream can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms that can contribute to gas and discomfort.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to soothe and protect the digestive tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate gas and bloating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with gas in stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. The essential oil extracted from lemongrass contains citral, which has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. Additionally, lemongrass has been found to enhance digestion and reduce bloating, making it an effective remedy for alleviating gas and discomfort in the stomach. Regular use of lemongrass creams can provide long-lasting relief.
remedy_constituents: Monoterpenes: They help with gas in the stomach by reducing inflammation and improving digestion, thus alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
Aldehydes (Citral): These compounds exhibit carminative properties, which help to release trapped gas in the digestive tract, providing relief from abdominal cramps and bloating.

Phenolic Compounds (Limonoids): They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating gas and discomfort in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high oil content, particularly limonene and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Rhyzomes: They are used because they contain citral, a natural compound with carminative and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relieve gas and stomach discomfort.
Stems: They are used for the same reason as rhyzomes and leaves, as they also contain essential oils with similar properties that help to soothe the stomach and alleviate gas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from a clean and well-maintained environment.
Extract the essential oil from the harvested leaves using a steam distillation process for 2 hours.
Combine the extracted essential oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to a storage container for later use.

remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric creams contain curcuminoids, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of gas and bloating.

Carminative properties: Turmeric has carminative properties, which means it helps to prevent the formation of gas bubbles in the stomach and intestines, reducing the discomfort caused by belching, bloating, and flatulence.

Antispasmodic properties: Turmeric creams also contain antispasmodic compounds that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to gas and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with gas in stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties and natural digestive enzymes. The curcumin present in turmeric creams works to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can cause gas and bloating. Additionally, turmeric creams may help to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). As a result, using turmeric creams topically may provide relief from stomach discomfort and gas, promoting overall digestive health and well-being. remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a phenolic compound, helps with gas in the stomach by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of enzymes that break down the lining of the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another phenolic compound found in Curcuma longa, aids in gas relief by blocking the action of certain enzymes involved in gas production, such as nitric oxide synthase, which contributes to stomach discomfort.

Turmerone (α- and β-Turmerone): Turmerone, a sesquiterpene, assists in gas relief by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting the relaxation of the muscles in the stomach, thus easing the passage of gas and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root of Curcuma longa is used to make creams for gas in the stomach because it has been traditionally used to aid digestion and relieve flatulence.
remedy_recipe: Extract 5 grams of curcuma longa rhizomes using a solvent like coconut oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of shea butter in a heat-resistant bowl over low heat.
Combine 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of essential oil of orange in a separate bowl.
Blend the curcuma longa extract with the beeswax mixture and stir until it reaches a creamy consistency in 10 minutes.
Add the vitamin e oil mixture and essential oil mixture to the curcuma longa cream and stir well for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to gas and bloating.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of chamazulene and bisabolol, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate stomach discomfort.
Leaves: They are used for their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and bloating in the stomach by relaxing the muscles and improving digestion.
Seeds: They are used for their spasmolytic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with gas and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and 500ml of a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Infuse the dried flowers in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine 50g of the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 50g of cocoa butter in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat until the beeswax and cocoa butter have melted completely and the mixture is smooth.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, which helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of bloating and gas.

Isoliquiritigenin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate gas-related discomfort.
Liquiritigenin: Has a direct effect on the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and helping to relieve gas and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate stomach gas and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce gas.
Barks: The barks are used for their ability to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, providing relief from gas and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin for 2 hours.
Filter the extract to remove the solid residues using a buchner funnel for 30 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the filtered extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture at 60-70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Pour the mixture into sterile containers and let it cool at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Carminative and Antispasmodic Properties: Dill cream's carminative properties help to release trapped gas from the digestive system, while its antispasmodic properties relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps and spasms associated with bloating.

Digestive Enzyme Stimulant Property: Dill cream contains compounds that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, such as amylase, lipase, and trypsin, which help to break down food more efficiently, reducing gas and discomfort in the stomach.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of anethum graveolens dried flowers and leaves and add to a glass mixing bowl.
Combine 100 grams of shea butter and 50 grams of coconut oil in a separate mixing bowl.
Melt the shea butter and coconut oil mixture in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 15 minutes.
Mix the anethum graveolens extract with the melted shea butter and coconut oil mixture for 5 minutes.
Pour the herbal mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Commiphora resin: Commiphora resin, a main component of Balm of Gilead, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with gas.

This helps to alleviate discomfort and pain associated with gas and bloating. remedy_constituents: Salicin: A phenolic glycoside that has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate gas and bloating symptoms.
Betulin: A triterpenoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect the stomach lining and reduce gas and discomfort.
Populin: A phenolic glycoside that has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate gas and bloating symptoms in the stomach.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried populus balsamifera bark in a sustainable manner.
Infuse 250 grams of the bark in 1 liter of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a glass container and discard the solids.
Combine the infused oil with 200 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Emulsify the mixture at 40 degrees celsius for 30 minutes and let it cool before pouring into a container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort associated with gas.

Digestive enzymes inhibitors: Sage contains compounds like camphor and borneol, which have been shown to inhibit digestive enzymes responsible for breaking down food, allowing for improved digestion and reduced gas production.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with gas in stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties and carminative effects. Additionally, the carminative properties of sage help to expel gas from the stomach, providing quick relief from uncomfortable symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the leaves in a warm place at 40°c for 2 hours or until they crumble.
Infuse 20g of dried leaves in 500ml of oil at 60°c for 2 hours in a double boiler.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 200g of beeswax and 50g of emulsifying wax in a heat-proof bowl.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with gas in stomach because they contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds. The cream's main ingredient, Marrubium vulgare, has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach. When applied topically to the abdomen, the cream can help ease discomfort and bloating caused by trapped gas, allowing for natural digestion and relief from symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Ginger: Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol that may help soothe the stomach and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds that help to relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated tissues and reduce inflammation in the stomach, alleviating gas and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Collect one ounce of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for five minutes.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 10 grams of beeswax, 5 grams of coconut oil, and 20 drops of lavender essential oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-resistant glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water and melt the beeswax and coconut oil for ten minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 100°f before whipping it with an electric mixer for five minutes until it thickens into a creamy consistency.
Pour the herbal ginkgo biloba cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months, allowing it to solidify at room temperature for thirty minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow creams helps with gas in stomach because of its soothing properties that ease digestive discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry malva sylvestris flowers in a well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried malva sylvestris flowers with two parts sweet almond oil and coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the oils to infuse with the herbs' properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for three days before whipping it with a hand mixer to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves creams helps with gas in stomach because of their unique properties that work to alleviate digestive discomfort. Digestive enzyme stimulants: Clove cream contains compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of bloating, flatulence, and discomfort caused by undigested food particles.

remedy_recipe: Dry 50 grams of fresh syzygium aromaticum bark in a low-temperature oven at 30°c for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of grated coconut oil with 5 grams of beeswax pellets and 20 grams of distilled water in a double boiler.
Add the syzygium aromaticum powder to the mixture in step 3 and stir until well combined for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and allow it to cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping into a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Grind dried illicium verum leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.

Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate absorption.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding any solids, and let it cool completely at room temperature.
Store the herbal illicium verum cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two weeks or freeze for longer storage.

remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of dried carum carvi seeds with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until melted and smooth.
Add the infused carum carvi liquid, 2 teaspoons of glycerin, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the melted wax and oil mixture, stirring well.
Pour the combined mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_recipe: Grind 2 teaspoons of dried elettaria cardamomum pods into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground cardamom powder with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 120°f to 130°f.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk for 5 minutes until it thickens.
Pour the cooled cream into a sterilized jar and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or freeze for up to 6 months.
